layers.name: Qt GUI Application

layers.name: Spatial Object Database

layers.name: Geometry Core & Algorithms
layers.description: Open3D-based cloudViewer primitives and repository-wide numeric/geometry/octree kernels under core/ reused by reconstruction, I/O, and filters.

layers.name: Scene Reconstruction

layers.name: Viewer Infrastructure & Serialization

layers.name: Build System & CI/CD

tour.title: Executable Entry Point
tour.description: The Qt application launches from here: command-line parsing, application setup, plugin discovery, and the hand-off into the GUI shell occur along this path. Reading `main.cpp` first shows how configuration, logging paths, and the main event loop bootstrap before any 3D data appears on screen.
tour.languageLesson: In CMake-based Qt/VTK stacks, "main" is often thin orchestration—the heavy UI and rendering types stay in separate translation units linked into the executable target wired by the root CMake project.

tour.title: Product Context
tour.description: The repository README summarizes ACloudViewer as a toolkit for CloudCompare-derived workflows with VTK visualization, reconstruction, Gaussian splatting, and Python bindings. Use it as an orientation layer before navigating `app/` and `libs/`, mapping marketing bullets to concrete source trees.

tour.title: GUI Shell & Main Workflow
tour.description: Together these define and implement `MainWindow`: the central `QMainWindow`, menus and actions, the database browser, multi-view docking, picking, plugin wiring, and file-driven workflows from the menus you actually click. Much of what looks like "the viewer" behavior is coordinated here rather than scattered ad hoc.

tour.title: File I/O Boundary
tour.description: Before entities exist in memory, loaders pass through `FileIOFilter` and related formats. Understanding this abstraction explains how toolbar "Open" actions reach concrete readers, how plugins add formats, and where you hook new point-cloud or polygon encodings without rewriting the GUI shell.

tour.title: Spatial Database Foundations
tour.description: `ecvHObject` anchors the hierarchical scene-graph model (transforms, parent/child containment, traversal). `ecvPointCloud` is the everyday concrete subtype for unstructured 3D samples. These headers establish the naming and lifecycle patterns echoed across sensor types, meshes, and octrees in `libs/CV_db/`.

tour.title: Drawing & View Routing
tour.description: `ecvDrawContext` expresses what each draw pass needs—state and parameters used when painting entities. `ecvViewManager` ties those draws to VTK-aware views (which window owns which camera, synchronization, refreshes after edits). Pairing both explains how edits in the DB tree ultimately schedule renders on the VTK canvas.

tour.title: Primary VTK GL View
tour.description: `vtkGLView` is the façade over the VTK render window/interactor used by the GUI. Inspect it after the DB-layer headers to bridge "what needs drawing" (`ecv*`) with "how VTK actually rasterizes/OpenGL composites" beneath Qt.

tour.title: VTK Display Helpers
tour.description: The header declares the façade for turning database entities into VTK actors and managing draw-heavy concerns; the source file is large because it embodies most cross-cutting visualization glue inside `libs/VtkEngine/`. Together they are the choke point between CloudCompare-era object models and VTK pipelines.

tour.title: Chart / Parallel Coordinates Views
tour.description: `vtkChartView` extends VTK with ParaView-style charting primitives (axes, XY plots), complementing purely spatial render views. Returning here after mastering `vtkGLView` clarifies why ACloudViewer can mix scientific plots with immersive 3D displays without bolting unrelated UI frameworks atop Qt.

tour.title: Tensor-Centric Geometry Core
tour.description: The geometry stack (`libs/cloudViewer/`) adopts Open3D-inspired tensor-heavy APIs. Start with `Tensor.h`—it expresses batched numerical storage for points, normals, colors, masks, etc., which propagate through algorithms and pipelines under `CVViewer/` without manual pointer juggling.
tour.languageLesson: Tensor-first C++ kernels mirror PyTorch/Open3D ergonomics—fixed rank small tensors unify CPU/GPU backends and simplify Python exposures later in the bind layer.

tour.title: COLMAP Reconstruction Model
tour.description: This header embodies the persisted reconstruction graph (cameras, images, points) used by bundled COLMAP-derived tooling. Connecting it mentally to `libs/Reconstruction/` clarifies why some viewers show sparse matches or bundle-adjusted landmarks alongside raw clouds loaded through `CV_io`.

tour.title: Plugin Contracts
tour.description: `ecvPluginInterface` typifies every dynamic extension: deterministic discovery from `plugins/`, guarded loading, standardized slots/menus/toolbars surfaced through `CVPluginStub` wrappers. Returning here after GUIs completes the narrative from monolithic exe to externally contributed vertical features.

tour.title: Python Bind Surface
tour.description: The pybind11 registration unit wires `cloudViewer` C++ primitives into Python, enabling scripted notebooks, tooling, or agent automation without recompiling the desktop binary. Seeing it immediately after tensors and reconstruction explains which native types downstream Python examples actually marshal.
tour.languageLesson: Modern pybind prefers minimal `PYBIND11_MODULE` shims splitting declarations across namespaces so incremental extensions add source files plus CMake targets without touching every binding.

tour.title: CMake Superbuild
tour.description: `CMakeLists.txt` at the repo root enumerates toolchain flags, fetched third parties (VTK, Qt, CUDA, Python), and subdirectory wiring for `core/`, `app/`, `libs/`, and `plugins/`. Treat this as your map whenever you chase link errors or conditional features toggled via cache variables.
tour.languageLesson: Superbuild CMake hierarchies propagate `INTERFACE`/`PUBLIC` imported targets outward so consumers inherit include dirs/transitive deps—a pattern that keeps VTK+Qt+MOC combinations manageable.

tour.title: CI Container Recipe
tour.description: `Dockerfile.ci` encodes repeatable Linux builders for GitHub-hosted CI: compilers, Conan/Conda stubs, xvfb stubs, caching layers, etc. Matching this file against workflow YAML under `.github/` shows how merges stay green despite the heavyweight dependency footprint implied by CMake.
tour.languageLesson: CI Dockerfiles reorder layers from slow-changing toolchain bases to volatile repository checkouts—maximizing buildkit cache reuse on every PR.
